- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

7098	# quality gate. Codex pass-3 CONDITIONAL-no-blockers revision:
7099	# reject rows containing the observed run-2 bad patterns
7100	# (fragment comparators and result-field placeholders), but
7101	# scope narrowly to avoid over-rejecting legitimate rows.
7102	_M66_FRAGMENT_COMPARATOR_RE = re.compile(
7103	    r"\s+in\s+adults\s+with\s+type\s*$",  # truncated NEJM/Lancet
7104	                                           # population boilerplate
7105	    re.IGNORECASE,

7109	def _m66_row_passes_quality_gate(cells: dict[str, str]) -> bool:
7110	    """V30 Phase-2 M-66 run-3 Trial Summary quality gate.
7111	
7112	    Rejects rows whose cells show observed run-2 failure modes:
7113	
7114	    1. comparator ends in "in adults with type" (truncated
7115	       NEJM/Lancet boilerplate like "insulin glargine in adults
7116	       with type"). Legitimate comparators like "semaglutide 1 mg"
7117	       or "insulin glargine" pass.
7118	    2. The effect cell is empty AND the fallback would render as
7119	       bare "at week N" placeholder with no numeric information.
7120	       Legitimate rows have either a real effect string (e.g.
7121	       "-0.45%") OR both a timepoint + some numeric population /
7122	       baseline info already shown in other cells.
7123	
7124	    Returns True to keep the row, False to reject.
7125	
7126	    Scoped narrowly per Codex pass-3 guidance so legitimate rows
7127	    with partial information survive.
7128	    """
7129	    comparator = (cells.get("comparator") or "").strip()
7130	    if _M66_FRAGMENT_COMPARATOR_RE.search(comparator):
7131	        return False
7132	
7133	    effect = (cells.get("effect") or "").strip()
7134	    timepoint = (cells.get("timepoint") or "").strip()
7135	    # If effect is missing AND timepoint is the only other non-
7136	    # empty cell in {baseline, effect, timepoint}, the rendered
7137	    # result becomes `at week {timepoint}` with no digits, which
7138	    # is the observed run-2 junk pattern. Legitimate rows with
7139	    # an effect OR with real baseline info are unaffected.
7140	    if not effect:
7141	        baseline = (cells.get("baseline") or "").strip()
7142	        n = (cells.get("n") or "").strip()
7143	        # Require at least one other numeric cell for a timepoint-
7144	        # only row to survive.
7145	        has_other_numeric = any(
7146	            bool(re.search(r"\d", cell)) for cell in (baseline, n)
7147	        )
7148	        if timepoint and not has_other_numeric:
7149	            return False
7150	
7151	    return True

7154	def _m42b_extract_from_quote(quote: str) -> dict[str, str]:
7155	    """Extract 7 frame-element cells from a direct_quote string.
7156	    Returns dict with keys {n, baseline, comparator, dose, endpoint,
7157	    timepoint, effect}. Missing fields are empty strings."""
7158	    if not quote:
7159	        return {k: "" for k in
7160	                ("n", "baseline", "comparator", "dose", "endpoint",
7161	                 "timepoint", "effect")}
7162	    cells: dict[str, str] = {}
7163	
7164	    m = _M42B_PAT_N.search(quote)
7165	    if m:
7166	        cells["n"] = m.group(1) or m.group(2) or ""
7167	    else:
7168	        cells["n"] = ""
7169	
7170	    m = _M42B_PAT_BASELINE.search(quote)
7171	    cells["baseline"] = (m.group(1).strip() if m else "")
7172	
7173	    m = _M42B_PAT_COMPARATOR.search(quote)
7174	    cells["comparator"] = (m.group(1).strip() if m else "")
7175	
7176	    m = _M42B_PAT_DOSE.search(quote)
7177	    cells["dose"] = (m.group(1).strip() if m else "")
7178	
7179	    m = _M42B_PAT_ENDPOINT.search(quote)
7180	    cells["endpoint"] = (m.group(1).strip() if m else "")
7181	
7182	    m = _M42B_PAT_TIMEPOINT.search(quote)
7183	    cells["timepoint"] = (m.group(1) or m.group(2) or "") if m else ""
7184	
7185	    m = _M42B_PAT_EFFECT_WITH_UNCERTAINTY.search(quote)
7186	    cells["effect"] = (m.group(1).strip() if m else "")

7193	    pass-2 medium). Returns 'yyyy' or empty string."""

7195	    # Common DOI/URL year patterns: /2021/, (2021), -2021-
7196	    m = re.search(r"[/(\-_](20\d{2})[/)\-_.]", url)

7212	    """Return the [N] citation number from the bibliography that
7213	    corresponds to this evidence row. Match by evidence_id or URL."""

7215	    url = row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or ""
7216	    for entry in bibliography:

7218	            return entry.get("num")

7224	def build_trial_summary_and_timeline_from_evidence(
7225	    selected_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
7226	    primary_trial_anchors: list[str],
7227	    bibliography: list[dict[str, Any]],
7228	    refetch_fn: Any = None,
7229	    *,
7230	    refetch_diagnostics_sink: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
7231	) -> tuple[str, str]:
7232	    """M-42b deterministic builder. Consumes selected evidence rows
7233	    (from the generator's evidence_pool) + the sweep's
7234	    `primary_trial_anchors` list + global bibliography. Returns
7235	    `(trial_table_md, timeline_md)` — both markdown strings.
7236	
7237	    Source-content contract (per Codex plan review pass-3):
7238	      - Primary extraction source: `row.get("direct_quote")` — the
7239	        verbatim quote populated by live_retriever during fetch.
7240	      - Secondary: `row.get("statement")` for disambiguation only.
7241	      - Forbidden: prose from any generated report section.
7242	
7243	    Thin-content fallback:
7244	      - If `direct_quote` < 100 chars AND `refetch_fn` is provided,
7245	        calls `refetch_fn(url)` to fetch a fresh 2000-char extract.
7246	      - If still thin, the row is marked extraction-ineligible
7247	        (skipped).
7248	
7249	    Row acceptance:
7250	      - For the TABLE: >=4 of 7 frame cells populated.
7251	      - For the TIMELINE: publication year + trial name + at least
7252	        one non-empty cell (endpoint OR effect).
7253	
7254	    Returns empty strings when no rows pass the threshold (caller
7255	    falls back to LLM path).
7256	    """
7257	    if not selected_rows or not primary_trial_anchors:
7258	        return "", ""
7259	
7260	    valid_ref_nums = {
7261	        int(e.get("num")) for e in bibliography
7262	        if isinstance(e.get("num"), int)
7263	    }
7264	
7265	    # Per-anchor: find the best primary row + extract cells
7266	    table_rows: list[tuple[int, str, dict[str, str], str]] = []
7267	    # format: (year_int, trial_name, cells, ref_marker)
7268	
7269	    for anchor in primary_trial_anchors:
7270	        anchor_l = anchor.lower()
7271	        # Find the first selected row whose title contains this anchor
7272	        # AND is a primary (M-42e would have tagged it at selection
7273	        # time — but the builder can't assume that metadata is
7274	        # exposed; we re-test here via title + URL). M-48 pass-2:
7275	        # live rows use `statement` not `title`.
7276	        best_row = None
7277	        for row in selected_rows:
7278	            title_text = ""
7279	            for k in ("title", "statement", "source_title"):
7280	                v = row.get(k)
7281	                if isinstance(v, str) and v:
7282	                    title_text = v
7283	                    break
7284	            if anchor_l in title_text.lower():
7285	                best_row = row
7286	                break
7287	        if best_row is None:
7288	            continue
7289	
7290	        # Source content: direct_quote primary, refetch fallback,
7291	        # SKIP if still thin. M-42b pass-2 (Codex audit blocker #1):
7292	        # pre-pass-2 used `statement` as an additional fallback, which
7293	        # violated the pass-3 source-content contract (statement is
7294	        # for disambiguation only, never as a standalone extraction
7295	        # source). Contract is now refetch-or-skip.
7296	        # M-45 (2026-04-22): when refetch_diagnostics_sink is provided,
7297	        # use the diagnostic-capable refetch variant so the orchestrator
7298	        # can emit refetch_diagnostics.json per Codex pass-2 acceptance.
7299	        quote = best_row.get("direct_quote") or ""
7300	        if len(quote) < 100 and refetch_fn is not None:
7301	            url = best_row.get("source_url") or best_row.get("url") or ""
7302	            # M-45 pass-2 (Codex audit medium #2): record skipped
7303	            # primary rows that have no refetchable URL so the
7304	            # diagnostic artifact covers every skipped primary row.
7305	            if not url and refetch_diagnostics_sink is not None:
7306	                refetch_diagnostics_sink.append({
7307	                    "url": "",
7308	                    "anchor": anchor,
7309	                    "evidence_id": best_row.get("evidence_id", ""),
7310	                    "attempted": False,
7311	                    "method": "none",
7312	                    "raw_char_count": len(quote),
7313	                    "body_type": "",
7314	                    "eligible": False,
7315	                    "failure_mode": "missing_url",
7316	                    "exception_type": "",
7317	                })
7318	            if url:
7319	                try:
7320	                    # M-45: if sink provided, route through the
7321	                    # diagnostic variant for per-URL telemetry.
7322	                    if refetch_diagnostics_sink is not None:
7323	                        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.live_retriever import (
7324	                            refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics,
7325	                        )
7326	                        refetched, diag = refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics(
7327	                            url, 2000,
7328	                        )
7329	                        diag["anchor"] = anchor
7330	                        diag["evidence_id"] = best_row.get("evidence_id", "")
7331	                        refetch_diagnostics_sink.append(diag)
7332	                    else:
7333	                        refetched = refetch_fn(url, 2000)
7334	                    if refetched and len(refetched) >= 100:
7335	                        quote = refetched
7336	                        # Cache on row for future access (also used
7337	                        # by _m42b_year_from_row in pass-2 medium fix).
7338	                        best_row["_m42b_refetched_quote"] = refetched
7339	                except Exception as exc:
7340	                    if refetch_diagnostics_sink is not None:
7341	                        refetch_diagnostics_sink.append({
7342	                            "url": url[:200],
7343	                            "anchor": anchor,
7344	                            "evidence_id": best_row.get("evidence_id", ""),

7652	def _m50_select_candidate_trials(
7653	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
7654	    primary_ev_ids_by_anchor: dict[str, list[str]],
7655	    bibliography: list[dict[str, Any]],
7656	    direct_anchors: set[str],
7657	) -> list[tuple[str, dict[str, Any], int, str]]:
7658	    """M-50 (2026-04-22): select candidate trials for per-trial
7659	    subsections.
7660	
7661	    Returns list of (anchor, primary_row, biblio_num, quote) tuples
7662	    for every anchor that:
7663	      - is in the T2D-direct set (passed by caller via direct_anchors)
7664	      - has ≥1 M-42e-detected primary ev_id in the pool
7665	      - the primary has a direct_quote OR refetched quote ≥100 chars
7666	        (strict contract preserved)
7667	      - the primary ev_id has a matching bibliography entry
7668	
7669	    The `quote` element is the richer of direct_quote / refetched
7670	    (M-47 pass-2 + M-50 pass-2 per Codex audit): length-based select
7671	    so a thin direct_quote does NOT short-circuit the richer refetch.
7672	
7673	    If fewer than _M50_MIN_PRIMARIES_FOR_SUBSECTIONS qualify, returns
7674	    empty list (strict gating — no subsections emitted).
7675	    """
7676	    candidates: list[tuple[str, dict[str, Any], int, str]] = []
7677	    biblio_by_evid: dict[str, int] = {}
7678	    for entry in bibliography:
7679	        evid = entry.get("evidence_id")
7680	        num = entry.get("num")
7681	        if isinstance(evid, str) and isinstance(num, int):
7682	            biblio_by_evid[evid] = num
7683	
7684	    for anchor, ev_ids in primary_ev_ids_by_anchor.items():
7685	        if anchor not in direct_anchors:
7686	            continue  # skip SURMOUNT-1/3/4 (indirect) and any other
7687	        for ev_id in ev_ids:
7688	            row = evidence_pool.get(ev_id)
7689	            if not row:
7690	                continue
7691	            # Pick the RICHER of direct_quote / refetched.
7692	            # M-50 pass-2 (Codex audit blocker): plain `a or b`
7693	            # short-circuits on any non-empty string, so a thin
7694	            # direct_quote would hide a fat refetched quote from
7695	            # downstream `_call_m50_per_trial_subsection`. Carry the
7696	            # selected quote through the candidate tuple so the
7697	            # LLM generator uses the exact same string we validated.
7698	            dq = row.get("direct_quote") or ""
7699	            rq = row.get("_m42b_refetched_quote") or ""
7700	            # Length-based selection: prefer the longer eligible one.
7701	            if len(rq) > len(dq) and len(rq) >= 100:
7702	                quote = rq
7703	            elif len(dq) >= 100:
7704	                quote = dq
7705	            elif len(rq) >= 100:
7706	                quote = rq
7707	            else:
7708	                continue  # neither ≥100 → strict-contract skip
7709	            biblio_num = biblio_by_evid.get(ev_id)
7710	            if not isinstance(biblio_num, int):
7711	                continue
7712	            candidates.append((anchor, row, biblio_num, quote))
7713	            break  # one primary per anchor
7714	
7715	    if len(candidates) < _M50_MIN_PRIMARIES_FOR_SUBSECTIONS:
7716	        return []
7717	    return candidates

7856	    weighted-enrichment selection only (``weighted_enrichment.diagnose_unbound_supports_selection``

7882	def _remap_section_markers_to_global(
7883	    section_results: list[SectionResult],
7884	    global_biblio: list[dict[str, Any]],
7885	) -> list[str]:
7886	    """Rewrite each section's [N] markers from section-local to global.
7887	
7888	    Returns a list of remapped section prose strings.
7889	
7890	    I-deepfix-002 (#1363): this NEVER drops a marker. Every [N] in already
7891	    strict_verify-PASSED section prose maps to its global number; off-topic
7892	    cite-suppression is handled upstream at the enrichment selection only, so a
7893	    verified section citation is never orphaned here."""
7894	    ev_to_global = {b["evidence_id"]: b["num"] for b in global_biblio}
7895	    remapped: list[str] = []
7896	    for sect in section_results:
7897	        if not sect.verified_text:
7898	            continue
7899	        # Build a mapping section-local-num -> global-num
7900	        local_to_global: dict[int, int] = {}
7901	        for entry in sect.biblio_slice:
7902	            local_num = entry.get("num")
7903	            ev_id = entry.get("evidence_id", "")
7904	            global_num = ev_to_global.get(ev_id)
7905	            if local_num is not None and global_num is not None:
7906	                local_to_global[local_num] = global_num
7907	        text = sect.verified_text
7908	
7909	        # Replace [N] markers using the mapping. Do the replace with a
7910	        # callable to avoid subsequent substitutions clobbering each
7911	        # other (e.g., [1] -> [5] -> [15]).
7912	        def _replace(match: re.Match) -> str:
7913	            n = int(match.group(1))
7914	            g = local_to_global.get(n)
7915	            return f"[{g}]" if g else match.group(0)

7960	def _section_dedup_similarity_threshold() -> float:
7961	    """Configured difflib similarity threshold (default 0.80). A malformed value falls back to the
7962	    default (fail-safe: never collapses on a bad env value)."""
7963	    raw = os.environ.get(_ENV_SECTION_DEDUP_SIMILARITY, "")

7987	def _section_body_is_near_duplicate(
7988	    candidate_body: str,
7989	    section_results: "list[SectionResult]",
7990	) -> "Optional[str]":
7991	    """Return the TITLE of the first already-assembled, non-dropped section whose normalized body is
7992	    >= the configured similarity threshold to ``candidate_body`` (else ``None``).
7993	
7994	    Content-only comparison (normalized per ``_normalize_section_body_for_dedup``) so the numbered
7995	    "Evidence base" body matches its joined-prose "Corroborated Weighted Findings" twin despite the
7996	    different formatting. Returns ``None`` when the flag is OFF, the candidate normalizes empty, or
7997	    nothing crosses the threshold."""
7998	    if not section_dedup_enabled():
7999	        return None
8000	    cand_norm = _normalize_section_body_for_dedup(candidate_body)
8001	    if not cand_norm:
8002	        return None
8003	    threshold = _section_dedup_similarity_threshold()
8004	    matcher = difflib.SequenceMatcher()
8005	    matcher.set_seq2(cand_norm)
8006	    for sr in section_results:
8007	        if getattr(sr, "dropped_due_to_failure", False):
8008	            continue
8009	        existing_norm = _normalize_section_body_for_dedup(getattr(sr, "verified_text", "") or "")
8010	        if not existing_norm:
8011	            continue
8012	        matcher.set_seq1(existing_norm)
8013	        # Cheap length prefilter (difflib's own guard) before the O(n*m) ratio.
8014	        if matcher.real_quick_ratio() < threshold or matcher.quick_ratio() < threshold:
8015	            continue
8016	        if matcher.ratio() >= threshold:
8017	            return getattr(sr, "title", "") or "<untitled>"
8018	    return None

8051	def _suppress_uncited_decimal_sentences(text: str) -> "tuple[str, list[str]]":
8052	    """Return ``(cleaned_text, removed_sentences)`` — every SENTENCE that carries a decimal number but
8053	    no in-bounds ``[N]`` / ``[#ev:]`` citation is removed (I-deepfix-006 PT11, suppress-only).
8054	
8055	    Splits ``text`` into sentences with external_evaluator's abbreviation-aware boundary helper (the
8056	    SAME unit the PT11 rule scores), so an abbreviation period never splits a sentence. A decimal is
8057	    detected AFTER stripping ``[#ev:…]`` tokens (their integer offsets are not empirical decimals), and
8058	    the citation test reads the RAW sentence (an ``[#ev:]`` provenance token OR an ``[N]`` marker both
8059	    count as cited). Pure, no-network, faithfulness-neutral. Returns the input UNCHANGED (and an empty
8060	    removed list) when nothing qualifies. Fail-safe: if the boundary helper cannot be imported, the text
8061	    is returned untouched (never blind-drop). OFF (``PG_COMPOSE_NUMERIC_CITE_GUARANTEE``) => the input
8062	    is returned unchanged (byte-identical) regardless of content."""
8063	    if not text or not text.strip() or not _compose_numeric_cite_guarantee_enabled():
8064	        return text, []
8065	    try:
8066	        from src.polaris_graph.evaluator.external_evaluator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
8067	            _next_real_sentence_end,
8068	        )
8069	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — cannot split safely -> never suppress blind
8070	        return text, []
8071	    parts: list[str] = []
8072	    pos = 0
8073	    n = len(text)
8074	    while pos < n:
8075	        rel_end = _next_real_sentence_end(text[pos:])
8076	        if rel_end is None:
8077	            parts.append(text[pos:])
8078	            break
8079	        parts.append(text[pos:pos + rel_end])
8080	        pos += rel_end
8081	    kept: list[str] = []
8082	    removed: list[str] = []
8083	    for seg in parts:
8084	        has_citation = bool(_PT11_CITATION_RE.search(seg))
8085	        decimal_scan = _PT11_EV_TOKEN_RE.sub("", seg)
8086	        has_decimal = bool(_PT11_DECIMAL_RE.search(decimal_scan))
8087	        if has_decimal and not has_citation:
8088	            stripped = seg.strip()
8089	            if stripped:
8090	                removed.append(stripped)
8091	            continue
8092	        kept.append(seg)
8093	    if not removed:
8094	        return text, []
8095	    cleaned = re.sub(r"[ \t]{2,}", " ", "".join(kept)).strip()
8096	    return cleaned, removed

8972	def _m44_find_trial_mentions(
8973	    text: str,
8974	    primary_trial_anchors: list[str],
8975	) -> list[tuple[str, int, int]]:
8976	    """M-44 (2026-04-22): scan prose for named-trial tokens.
8977	
8978	    Returns list of (anchor, start_offset, end_offset) tuples. Uses
8979	    word-boundary regex so partial matches (e.g. 'SURPASS-10' wouldn't
8980	    match 'SURPASS-1' anchor) are avoided, but accepts colon/paren/
8981	    comma separators after the token.
8982	    """
8983	    if not text or not primary_trial_anchors:
8984	        return []
8985	    matches: list[tuple[str, int, int]] = []
8986	    for anchor in primary_trial_anchors:
8987	        # Word boundary at start; either word boundary OR punctuation
8988	        # at end to catch "SURPASS-2:" and "SURPASS-2)".
8989	        pattern = r"\b" + re.escape(anchor) + r"(?=[\s:;,.\)\]\-]|$)"
8990	        for m in re.finditer(pattern, text):
8991	            matches.append((anchor, m.start(), m.end()))
8992	    return matches

8995	def _m44_sentence_spans(text: str) -> list[tuple[int, int]]:
8996	    """Return (start, end) offsets for each sentence in `text`.
8997	    Simple split on .!? followed by whitespace or end-of-text."""
8998	    if not text:
8999	        return []
9000	    spans: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
9001	    start = 0
9002	    i = 0
9003	    while i < len(text):
9004	        ch = text[i]
9005	        if ch in ".!?" and (i + 1 >= len(text) or text[i + 1].isspace()):
9006	            # End of sentence.
9007	            spans.append((start, i + 1))
9008	            # Skip whitespace
9009	            j = i + 1
9010	            while j < len(text) and text[j].isspace():
9011	                j += 1
9012	            start = j
9013	            i = j
9014	        else:
9015	            i += 1
9016	    if start < len(text):
9017	        spans.append((start, len(text)))
9018	    return spans

9021	def _m44_validate_primary_same_sentence(
9022	    verified_text: str,
9023	    primary_ev_ids_by_anchor: dict[str, list[str]],
9024	    biblio_slice: list[dict[str, Any]],
9025	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
9026	    """M-44 (2026-04-22): same-sentence / adjacent-sentence validator.
9027	
9028	    Codex plan pass-2 verbatim: "For each named trial mentioned in the
9029	    section, if a matching M-42e primary ev_id is present in the
9030	    section subset, that primary ev_id must be cited in the same
9031	    sentence or immediately adjacent sentence."
9032	
9033	    Returns list of violations: [{anchor, trial_offset, sentence_text,
9034	    primary_ev_id_expected, citations_found}]. Empty list = validator
9035	    passes.
9036	
9037	    `biblio_slice` maps [N] marker numbers back to ev_ids. The
9038	    validator looks for `[N]` tokens in the same sentence as the
9039	    trial name; if none of them map to the expected primary ev_id,
9040	    it checks the next sentence; if still none, records a violation.
9041	    """
9042	    if not verified_text or not primary_ev_ids_by_anchor:
9043	        return []
9044	
9045	    # Build num→ev_id lookup
9046	    num_to_ev: dict[int, str] = {}
9047	    for entry in biblio_slice:
9048	        num = entry.get("num")
9049	        ev_id = entry.get("evidence_id")
9050	        if isinstance(num, int) and isinstance(ev_id, str):
9051	            num_to_ev[num] = ev_id
9052	
9053	    sentence_spans = _m44_sentence_spans(verified_text)
9054	    anchors = list(primary_ev_ids_by_anchor.keys())
9055	    mentions = _m44_find_trial_mentions(verified_text, anchors)
9056	
9057	    violations: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
9058	    for anchor, t_start, t_end in mentions:
9059	        expected_ev_ids = set(primary_ev_ids_by_anchor.get(anchor, []))
9060	        if not expected_ev_ids:
9061	            continue
9062	        # Find containing sentence
9063	        idx = None
9064	        for i, (s, e) in enumerate(sentence_spans):
9065	            if s <= t_start < e:
9066	                idx = i
9067	                break
9068	        if idx is None:
9069	            continue
9070	        # M-44 pass-2 (Codex audit finding #4): "immediately adjacent
9071	        # sentence" includes BOTH the previous and the following
9072	        # sentence. Pre-pass-2 only forward-checked, causing false
9073	        # violations when primary cite landed in the preceding
9074	        # sentence (a common writing pattern: "[1] In SURPASS-2,
9075	        # N=1879...").
9076	        check_ranges: list[tuple[int, int]] = [sentence_spans[idx]]
9077	        if idx - 1 >= 0:
9078	            check_ranges.append(sentence_spans[idx - 1])
9079	        if idx + 1 < len(sentence_spans):
9080	            check_ranges.append(sentence_spans[idx + 1])
9081	        citations_found: list[str] = []
9082	        for rs, re_ in check_ranges:
9083	            segment = verified_text[rs:re_]
9084	            for m in re.finditer(r"\[(\d+)\]", segment):
9085	                num = int(m.group(1))
9086	                ev_id = num_to_ev.get(num)
9087	                if ev_id:
9088	                    citations_found.append(ev_id)
9089	        hits = [e for e in citations_found if e in expected_ev_ids]
9090	        if not hits:
9091	            violations.append({
9092	                "anchor": anchor,
9093	                "trial_offset": t_start,
9094	                "sentence_text": verified_text[
9095	                    sentence_spans[idx][0]:sentence_spans[idx][1]
9096	                ],
9097	                "primary_ev_id_expected": list(expected_ev_ids),
9098	                "citations_found": citations_found,
9099	            })
9100	    return violations

9192	def _m47_extract_candidate_values(quote: str) -> list[tuple[str, float, str]]:
9193	    """M-47 (2026-04-22): extract candidate quantitative findings
9194	    from a clamp/PK paper's direct_quote.
9195	
9196	    Returns list of (field_name, numeric_value, unit_hint) tuples.
9197	    Empty list when quote contains no recognizable clamp/PK fields.
9198	    """
9199	    if not quote:
9200	        return []
9201	    out: list[tuple[str, float, str]] = []
9202	    text = quote.lower()
9203	    for pattern, field_name in _M47_VALUE_PATTERNS:
9204	        for m in re.finditer(pattern, text, flags=re.IGNORECASE):
9205	            try:
9206	                val = float(m.group(1))
9207	            except (ValueError, IndexError):
9208	                continue
9209	            # Capture unit-hint group if present (some patterns have it)
9210	            unit = ""
9211	            try:
9212	                if m.lastindex and m.lastindex >= 2:
9213	                    unit = (m.group(2) or "").lower()
9214	            except Exception:
9215	                unit = ""
9216	            out.append((field_name, val, unit))
9217	    # Deduplicate by (field_name, round(val, 2)) to collapse
9218	    # near-identical matches
9219	    seen: set[tuple[str, float]] = set()
9220	    dedup: list[tuple[str, float, str]] = []
9221	    for f, v, u in out:
9222	        key = (f, round(v, 2))
9223	        if key not in seen:
9224	            seen.add(key)
9225	            dedup.append((f, v, u))
9226	    return dedup

9268	def _m47_prose_contains_value(
9269	    section_text: str,
9270	    ev_id: str,
9271	    field_name: str,
9272	    expected_value: float,
9273	    tolerance_pct: float = 5.0,
9274	    biblio_slice: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
9275	) -> bool:
9276	    """M-47 (2026-04-22): check whether `section_text` contains a
9277	    reference to `expected_value` (within ±tolerance_pct%) in the
9278	    same sentence as a citation pointing to `ev_id` AND in the same
9279	    sentence as a field-context token for `field_name`.
9280	
9281	    M-47 pass-2 (Codex audit blocker #1): field-aware matching. The
9282	    sentence must contain both (a) a number within tolerance of the
9283	    expected value, AND (b) a field-context token (e.g. 'M-value'
9284	    for m_value_pct, 'half-life' for half_life). Pre-pass-2 matching
9285	    was value-only, so "63 participants" would false-pass an
9286	    M-value=63 extraction.
9287	
9288	    Unit normalization: half-life hours↔days (1 day = 24 hours).
9289	
9290	    `biblio_slice` maps [N] markers → ev_ids.
9291	    """
9292	    if not section_text or expected_value <= 0:
9293	        return False
9294	
9295	    # Build num → ev_id lookup
9296	    num_to_ev: dict[int, str] = {}
9297	    if biblio_slice:
9298	        for entry in biblio_slice:
9299	            num = entry.get("num")
9300	            eid = entry.get("evidence_id")
9301	            if isinstance(num, int) and isinstance(eid, str):
9302	                num_to_ev[num] = eid
9303	
9304	    # For half-life field: allow day↔hour equivalence
9305	    equiv_values = [expected_value]
9306	    if field_name == "half_life":
9307	        # 5 days = 120 hours; 120 hours = 5 days
9308	        equiv_values.append(expected_value * 24.0)  # days → hours
9309	        equiv_values.append(expected_value / 24.0)  # hours → days
9310	
9311	    context_tokens = _M47_FIELD_CONTEXT_TOKENS.get(field_name, ())
9312	
9313	    sentence_spans = _m44_sentence_spans(section_text)
9314	    for s, e in sentence_spans:
9315	        seg = section_text[s:e]
9316	        seg_lower = seg.lower()
9317	        # Does this sentence cite the target ev_id?
9318	        cited = False
9319	        if f"[{ev_id}]" in seg:
9320	            cited = True
9321	        if not cited and num_to_ev:
9322	            for m in re.finditer(r"\[(\d+)\]", seg):
9323	                if num_to_ev.get(int(m.group(1))) == ev_id:
9324	                    cited = True
9325	                    break
9326	        if not cited:
9327	            continue
9328	        # M-47 pass-2: also require field-context token in the same
9329	        # sentence. When no context_tokens configured for this field,
9330	        # fall through to value-only matching (backwards compat).
9331	        has_context = not context_tokens or any(
9332	            tok in seg_lower for tok in context_tokens
9333	        )
9334	        if not has_context:
9335	            continue
9336	        # Does this sentence contain a number within the expected range?
9337	        for m in re.finditer(r"(\d+\.?\d*)", seg):
9338	            try:
9339	                v = float(m.group(1))
9340	            except ValueError:
9341	                continue
9342	            for ev in equiv_values:
9343	                d = max(0.01, ev * tolerance_pct / 100.0)
9344	                if ev - d <= v <= ev + d:
9345	                    return True
9346	    return False

9349	def _m47_validate_mechanism_clamp_extraction(
9350	    verified_text: str,
9351	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
9352	    ev_ids_in_subset: list[str],
9353	    biblio_slice: list[dict[str, Any]],
9354	) -> dict[str, Any]:
9355	    """M-47 (2026-04-22): evidence-linked validator for Mechanism
9356	    section clamp/PK extraction.
9357	
9358	    Codex plan pass-2 verbatim: "The validator extracts candidate
9359	    quantitative fields from the cited clamp/PK evidence row's
9360	    direct_quote or accepted refetched quote, normalizes units/
9361	    patterns, and then checks that at least three of those same
9362	    values/fields appear in the verified Mechanism section with the
9363	    clamp/PK ev_id citation. Broad numeric counts in the section do
9364	    not satisfy the rule."
9365	
9366	    Returns diagnostic dict:
9367	      {
9368	        'clamp_papers_in_subset': list[ev_id],
9369	        'per_paper': {
9370	            ev_id: {
9371	                'candidate_fields': list[(field, value, unit)],
9372	                'matched_fields': list[(field, value)],
9373	                'match_count': int,
9374	                'passes_threshold': bool,  # ≥3
9375	            }
9376	        },
9377	        'any_passes_threshold': bool,  # any clamp paper met the floor
9378	        'no_clamp_papers': bool,  # True when subset has none (no-op)
9379	      }
9380	    """
9381	    result: dict[str, Any] = {
9382	        "clamp_papers_in_subset": [],
9383	        "per_paper": {},
9384	        "any_passes_threshold": False,
9385	        "no_clamp_papers": False,
9386	    }
9387	    if not verified_text or not evidence_pool or not ev_ids_in_subset:
9388	        result["no_clamp_papers"] = True
9389	        return result
9390	    clamp_papers: list[str] = []
9391	    for ev_id in ev_ids_in_subset:
9392	        row = evidence_pool.get(ev_id)
9393	        if row and _m47_row_is_clamp_or_pk_paper(row):
9394	            clamp_papers.append(ev_id)
9395	    result["clamp_papers_in_subset"] = clamp_papers
9396	    if not clamp_papers:
9397	        result["no_clamp_papers"] = True
9398	        return result
9399	
9400	    for ev_id in clamp_papers:
9401	        row = evidence_pool[ev_id]
9402	        # Source text: pick richer of direct_quote or refetched
9403	        # quote. M-47 pass-2 (Codex audit blocker #3): plain `a or b`
9404	        # short-circuits on any non-empty string, so a thin
9405	        # direct_quote hid a fat refetched quote.
9406	        dq = row.get("direct_quote") or ""
9407	        rq = row.get("_m42b_refetched_quote") or ""
9408	        if len(rq) > len(dq) and len(rq) >= 100:
9409	            quote = rq
9410	        elif len(dq) >= 100:
9411	            quote = dq
9412	        elif len(rq) >= 100:
9413	            quote = rq
9414	        else:
9415	            quote = dq or rq  # whatever we have; candidates will be empty
9416	        candidates = _m47_extract_candidate_values(quote)
9417	        matched: list[tuple[str, float]] = []
9418	        for field_name, val, _unit in candidates:
9419	            if _m47_prose_contains_value(
9420	                verified_text, ev_id, field_name, val,
9421	                biblio_slice=biblio_slice,
9422	            ):
9423	                matched.append((field_name, val))
9424	        passes = len(matched) >= 3
9425	        if passes:
9426	            result["any_passes_threshold"] = True
9427	        result["per_paper"][ev_id] = {
9428	            "candidate_fields": [
9429	                {"field": f, "value": v, "unit": u}
9430	                for f, v, u in candidates
9431	            ],
9432	            "matched_fields": [
9433	                {"field": f, "value": v} for f, v in matched
9434	            ],
9435	            "match_count": len(matched),
9436	            "passes_threshold": passes,
9437	        }
9438	    return result

9441	def _apply_atom_refusal_validation(
9442	    section_results: list["SectionResult"],
9443	    atom_mode: str,
9444	) -> tuple[int, int]:
9445	    """Post-hoc atom-refusal validation hook (PG_ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE).
9446	
9447	    Extracted from generate_multi_section_report so the strict-mode
9448	    fail-CLOSED behavior is directly unit-testable (F26).
9449	
9450	    Mutates each eligible SectionResult in place:
9451	      - log_only: records gap_records / counts; never touches verified_text.
9452	      - strict:   records counts AND replaces verified_text with the
9453	        validator's rendered_text (refusal blocks inline) when refusals fire,
9454	        AND decrements sentences_verified by that section's refusal_count so the
9455	        verified tally is HONEST (a refused sentence is no longer verified prose).
9456	
9457	    F26 (P2) — strict-mode fail-CLOSED:
9458	      The prior implementation was fail-OPEN: in strict mode an empty
9459	      atom_catalog was silently SKIPPED (mode="skipped_empty_catalog") and a
9460	      validator exception was swallowed for the WHOLE loop — either way a
9461	      refused/un-validatable section shipped as if it passed. Now, in strict
9462	      mode, an empty catalog OR a per-section validator exception marks THAT
9463	      section `atom_validation_degraded = True` (distinct, testable signal,
9464	      with no atom_validation_result so it stays out of the "validated" tally)
9465	      and the loop continues for the remaining sections (per-section isolation).
9466	      log_only keeps its original fail-soft semantics (advisory mode).
9467	
9468	    Returns (refusal_replacements, degraded_section_count).
9469	    """
9470	    refusal_replacements = 0
9471	    degraded_count = 0
9472	    if atom_mode not in ("log_only", "strict"):
9473	        return (refusal_replacements, degraded_count)
9474	
9475	    strict = atom_mode == "strict"
9476	    try:
9477	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.atom_refusal_validator import (
9478	            validate_section,
9479	        )
9480	    except Exception as _import_exc:
9481	        # Import failure: log_only stays fail-soft (advisory). strict
9482	        # fail-CLOSED — every eligible section is un-validatable, so mark
9483	        # each one degraded rather than silently shipping un-validated prose.
9484	        logger.warning(
9485	            "[multi_section] I-gen-005 Step 3b atom validator import failed: "
9486	            "%s (mode=%s)",
9487	            _import_exc,
9488	            atom_mode,
9489	        )
9490	        if strict:
9491	            for sr in section_results:
9492	                if sr.dropped_due_to_failure or not sr.verified_text:
9493	                    continue
9494	                sr.atom_validation_mode = "strict_degraded_import_error"
9495	                sr.atom_validation_degraded = True
9496	                degraded_count += 1
9497	        return (refusal_replacements, degraded_count)
9498	
9499	    for sr in section_results:
9500	        if sr.dropped_due_to_failure or not sr.verified_text:
9501	            continue
9502	        # Empty atom_catalog: the contract/V30 path (PG_V30_PHASE2_ENABLED)
9503	        # produces SectionResults without an atom catalog. Validating with an
9504	        # empty catalog would refuse EVERY claim sentence (false-positive
9505	        # storm), so we do NOT validate. But in strict mode that section is
9506	        # un-validatable -> fail-CLOSED: flag it degraded (NOT silent ok).
9507	        # log_only stays advisory: record the benign skip and move on.
9508	        if not sr.atom_catalog:
9509	            if strict:
9510	                sr.atom_validation_mode = "strict_degraded_empty_catalog"
9511	                sr.atom_validation_degraded = True
9512	                degraded_count += 1
9513	                logger.warning(
9514	                    "[multi_section] F26: strict-mode atom validation could "
9515	                    "NOT certify section %r (empty atom_catalog) — marked "
9516	                    "DEGRADED (fail-closed), not silently passed",
9517	                    sr.title,
9518	                )
9519	            else:
9520	                sr.atom_validation_mode = "skipped_empty_catalog"
9521	                logger.info(
9522	                    "[multi_section] I-gen-005 Step 3e: skipping atom "
9523	                    "validation for section %r (empty catalog)",
9524	                    sr.title,
9525	                )
9526	            continue
9527	        section_id = sr.title.lower().replace(" ", "_")
9528	        # Per-section isolation: a validator exception degrades ONLY this
9529	        # section (strict) and the loop continues. Previously the try wrapped
9530	        # the whole loop, so one section's failure aborted validation for ALL.
9531	        try:
9532	            val_result = validate_section(
9533	                sr.verified_text,
9534	                section_id=section_id,
9535	                section_title=sr.title,
9536	                catalog=sr.atom_catalog,
9537	            )
9538	        except Exception as _val_exc:
9539	            if strict:
9540	                sr.atom_validation_mode = "strict_degraded_validator_error"
9541	                sr.atom_validation_degraded = True
9542	                degraded_count += 1
9543	                logger.warning(
9544	                    "[multi_section] F26: strict-mode atom validation RAISED "
9545	                    "for section %r: %s — marked DEGRADED (fail-closed), not "
9546	                    "silently passed",
9547	                    sr.title,
9548	                    _val_exc,
9549	                )
9550	            else:
9551	                logger.warning(
9552	                    "[multi_section] I-gen-005 Step 3b atom validation raised "
9553	                    "for section %r (non-fatal, log_only): %s",
9554	                    sr.title,
9555	                    _val_exc,
9556	                )
9557	            continue
9558	        sr.atom_validation_result = val_result
9559	        sr.refusal_count = val_result.refusal_count
9560	        sr.soft_mismatch_count = val_result.soft_mismatch_count
9561	        sr.atom_validation_mode = atom_mode
9562	        if strict and val_result.refusal_count > 0:
9563	            sr.verified_text = val_result.rendered_text
9564	            refusal_replacements += val_result.refusal_count
9565	            # Honest count recompute (F26 + spec): a refused sentence was
9566	            # replaced with a refusal block, so it is no longer verified
9567	            # prose. Decrement this section's verified tally (clamped at 0)
9568	            # so the aggregate total_sentences_verified is not overcounted.
9569	            sr.sentences_verified = max(
9570	                0, sr.sentences_verified - val_result.refusal_count
9571	            )
9572	
9573	    logger.info(
9574	        "[multi_section] I-gen-005 Step 3b atom validation: mode=%s "
9575	        "sections_validated=%d refusal_replacements=%d degraded=%d",
9576	        atom_mode,
9577	        sum(1 for sr in section_results if sr.atom_validation_result),
9578	        refusal_replacements,
9579	        degraded_count,
9580	    )
9581	    return (refusal_replacements, degraded_count)

9584	def _apply_cross_section_repetition_guard(section_results: list["SectionResult"]) -> dict[str, Any]:
9585	    """I-deepfix-001 FIX 5 (#1344): the render-assembly-seam CALLER for the cross-section repetition
9586	    guard. Extracted from ``generate_multi_section_report`` (mirrors the ``_credibility_guard_decision``
9587	    extraction) so the fail-conservative + honest-marker contract is directly unit-testable without the
9588	    full async pipeline.
9589	
9590	    DEFAULT-OFF (``PG_CROSS_SECTION_REPETITION_GUARD`` unset / off token): returns ``{}`` and does
9591	    NOTHING — no snapshot, no marker, no mutation — so the assembled report is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the
9592	    legacy path.
9593	
9594	    ON: CONSOLIDATE a finding that recurs VERBATIM across DIFFERENT sections down to its richest instance
9595	    plus a citation-preserving back-reference (RENDER-ONLY / faithfulness-NEUTRAL — the module edits only
9596	    ``verified_text`` in place, AFTER the frozen faithfulness engine has run; see the module docstring),
9597	    emit the honest-liveness marker ``[activation] cross_section_repetition_guard: consolidated=<N>``
9598	    carrying the REALIZED count (``0`` = ran-ok-zero; NEVER gated on a >0 count per §-1.3), and return the
9599	    guard telemetry.
9600	
9601	    FAIL-CONSERVATIVE: any guard error restores each section's pre-guard ``verified_text`` (the guard only
9602	    ever does in-place substring swaps — it never drops a section; restore makes "keep the ORIGINAL
9603	    sections" literal even against a partial-apply), emits the DISTINCT
9604	    ``[activation] cross_section_repetition_guard: unavailable_failopen`` degrade marker the activation
9605	    canary REJECTS, and returns ``{}`` so assembly proceeds with the ORIGINAL sections."""
9606	    if not cross_section_repetition_guard_enabled():
9607	        return {}
9608	    # FAIL-CONSERVATIVE snapshot of each section's pre-guard verified_text.
9609	    _pre_guard = [(sr, getattr(sr, "verified_text", None)) for sr in section_results]
9610	    try:
9611	        telemetry = consolidate_cross_section_repetition(section_results)
9612	        consolidated = int((telemetry or {}).get("consolidated", 0) or 0)
9613	        logger.info(
9614	            "[activation] cross_section_repetition_guard: consolidated=%d",
9615	            consolidated,
9616	        )
9617	        return telemetry or {}
9618	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-conservative: restore + keep the original sections
9619	        for _sr, _orig in _pre_guard:
9620	            try:
9621	                _sr.verified_text = _orig
9622	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — best-effort restore; never let cleanup mask the degrade
9623	                pass
9624	        logger.warning(
9625	            "[activation] cross_section_repetition_guard: unavailable_failopen (%s)",
9626	            exc,
9627	        )
9628	        return {}
